web_results.title: The 2026 Guide to SaaS, AI, and Agentic Pricing Models
web_results.description: By tying price to usage, the company ensures revenue scales with the expensive workload. In fact, Monetizely’s Price to Scale book highlights an example of this logic: with an AI-based SaaS product, “the ongoing cost implications are non-trivial, which increases the need to select a usage-based pricing metric”. Early AI SaaS pioneers like OpenAI’s API, for instance, charged per thousand tokens (a usage metric) rather than a flat license fee, precisely because of the high variable cost per request.

web_results.title: The AI pricing and monetization playbook - Bessemer Venture Partners
web_results.description: 80-90% for SaaS. If the math doesn't work at 10 customers, it won't at 1,000. Track true costs from day one (including founder time) and design pricing that covers compute while capturing customer value.

web_results.title: Compare AI monetization solutions for SaaS (2026 edition)
web_results.description: Alguna acts as a complete revenue operations platform, capturing usage events (API calls, tokens, etc.) in real time, auto-invoicing customers, and syncing data with CRM and accounting tools. Known for fast deployment (customers go live in weeks), Alguna lets teams launch or iterate on pricing without code. Unlike legacy systems, it natively supports AI-era models like pay-per-use, AI agent billing, and hybrid subscription and consumption plans.

web_results.title: The Economics of AI-First B2B SaaS in 2026
web_results.description: Instead of 10-20% of revenue going to COGS (as in a typical SaaS), an AI SaaS might see 40-50% (or more) of revenue eaten by COGS in the form of model hosting, inference compute, and data costs.
